results.possibleIdentity: William W. Parker was an electrical engineer who was active during the late 1960s and early 1970s, particularly in the field of power electronics and technical collaboration with the arts. He was an employee of the Westinghouse Electric Corporation and was based in Newtown, Connecticut. Parker is recognized for his association with Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization founded in 1966 by Billy Klüver and Robert Rauschenberg to facilitate collaborations between artists and engineers. As a member of E.A.T., he participated in the organization's technical services program, which matched artists with engineers and scientists to provide technical assistance for artistic projects. His technical background included significant work in electrical engineering and power systems, and he is listed as an author or technical monitor on engineering documentation related to Westinghouse's power electronics projects. He may be the same William W. Parker who earned multiple degrees in electrical engineering from the University of Michigan (B.S. 1964, M.S. 1966, Ph.D. 1969) and later worked in the telecommunications and electronics industries for companies such as ITT and Bell Labs.
